cards.front: What ending do you attach to a vowel-ending stem for future tense?
cards.grammarNote: Attach ㄹ 거예요 after vowel-ending stems: 가다 → 갈 거예요, 보다 → 볼 거예요, 하다 → 할 거예요. The ㄹ is added as a final consonant on the stem's last syllable.

cards.front: What ending do you attach to a consonant-ending stem for future tense?
cards.grammarNote: Consonant-ending stems take 을 거예요: 먹다 → 먹을 거예요, 읽다 → 읽을 거예요. The 을 bridges the final consonant and 거예요.

cards.front: I will do / I will study (공부하다 — future)
I will meet a friend (만나다 — future)
I will rest at home (쉬다 — future)

cards.grammarNote: -(으)려고 해요 expresses deliberate intention or plan: consonant stem + 으려고 해요, vowel stem + 려고 해요. It is softer and more personal than -(으)ㄹ 거예요.

cards.front: I'm planning to study this evening
I won't be able to eat (못 negation)
